title: Post-Stress Repair Plan: Coordination, DuckDB, FK Audit, Evidence
author: orchestrator
summary: Repair plan addressing 5 blocking stress findings and 2 non-blocking validation findings identified during the stress-and-validation wave against the current working tree. Four repair phases (R1-R4) organized by dependency: coordination table migration + write tool fallback (R1), DuckDB subprocess hardening (R2), FK audit and path traversal fixes (R3), evidence and approval placeholders (R4).

critical.title: Coordination tables not in migration
critical.description: coordination/coordination.pg.sql.ts exists but was never added to the migration-pg/ DDL. The write tool calls checkPathReserved which queries the non-existent coordination_reservation table, blocking ALL writes in default SQLite configuration.

high.title: DuckDB run() accepts arbitrary SQL
high.description: The DuckDB adapter's run() passes arbitrary SQL to a subprocess without -readonly flag. Application-layer read-only enforcement only blocks Drizzle ORM methods, not raw SQL via run().

high.title: DuckDB spawns one subprocess per query
high.description: No connection pooling. N concurrent queries spawn N duckdb processes.

high.title: FK audit LIMIT 1000
high.description: migrate-to-pg.ts FK violation queries use LIMIT 1000. Tables with >1000 orphans only report the first 1000.

high.title: send_message path traversal
high.description: The recipient parameter in send-message.ts is not sanitized — allows ../ traversal.

existing_approvals.red_team_approval.v1.json: PLACEHOLDER — needs real content after stress pass
existing_approvals.repair_plan_approval.v1.json: PLACEHOLDER — needs real content after repair completion

repair_phases.name: Coordination Table Migration + Write Tool Fallback
repair_phases.description: Fix the blocking issue where coordination tables exist in coordination.pg.sql.ts but are not in the migration-pg/ DDL, causing write tool failures when checkPathReserved queries the non-existent coordination_reservation table.

items.title: Add coordination_reservation table to DDL or add fallback
items.approach: Two-path solution: (1) If migration-pg/ exists and coordination tables are missing, either re-run drizzle-kit generate to pick up coordination.pg.sql.ts, or CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS in the coordination tool's init path. (2) In write.ts and edit.ts, add graceful fallback when checkPathReserved fails — catch database errors and allow the write to proceed with a warning instead of blocking all writes.
files_changed: packages/opencode/migration-pg/ (re-run drizzle-kit generate or add coordination DDL manually)
items.acceptance: checkPathReserved no longer blocks writes when coordination_reservation table is missing. Either the table is created on startup, or write.ts/edit.ts have graceful fallback. Write operations succeed in default SQLite configuration.

repair_phases.name: DuckDB Subprocess Hardening
repair_phases.description: Harden the DuckDB sidecar against two vulnerabilities: (1) run() passes arbitrary SQL to duckdb subprocess without -readonly flag, allowing write operations through raw SQL that bypass application-layer guards. (2) Each query spawns a new subprocess with no connection pooling, causing resource exhaustion under concurrent load.

items.title: Add -readonly flag to duckdb subprocess spawn args
items.approach: Locate the duckdb subprocess spawn site in db.duckdb.ts or the DuckDB adapter. Add -readonly flag to the argument list so that any SQL injection or accidental write through run() is rejected at the database process level. This is a defense-in-depth measure complementing application-layer read-only enforcement.
items.acceptance: duckdb subprocess starts with -readonly flag. Write queries (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, CREATE, DROP) are rejected at the process level. Read queries (SELECT, EXPLAIN, DESCRIBE) succeed.

items.title: Add connection pooling or persistent subprocess to DuckDB
items.approach: Replace per-query subprocess spawn with a connection pool (maxConnections=4, idleTimeout=30000ms) in db.duckdb.ts that limits concurrent subprocesses to pool size. Queued queries wait for an available pool slot. Failed subprocesses are retried.
files_changed: packages/opencode/src/storage/adapter.ts (update DuckDBAdapter to use pooled connection)
items.acceptance: Pool parameters: maxConnections=4, idleTimeout=30000ms. 8 concurrent queries produce ≤4 duckdb processes. Pool recycles failed connections. typecheck and tests pass.

repair_phases.name: FK Audit LIMIT Fix + send_message Path Sanitization
repair_phases.description: Fix two medium-complexity blocking findings: (1) migrate-to-pg.ts FK violation queries use LIMIT 1000, hiding orphan counts beyond 1000. (2) send-message.ts recipient parameter allows ../ path traversal.

items.title: Remove LIMIT from FK audit queries, use COUNT-based detection
items.approach: In migrate-to-pg.ts, replace the LIMIT 1000 FK violation queries with COUNT-based detection. First query COUNT(*) to detect if any violations exist, then optionally query all violations for reporting (without LIMIT). This ensures all orphans are detected and reported, not just the first 1000 per table.
files_changed: packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/migrate-to-pg.ts (remove LIMIT from FK violation queries, add COUNT-based detection)
items.acceptance: FK audit detects violations beyond 1000 rows per table. COUNT query runs first. All violations reported if any exist. typecheck and tests pass.

items.title: Add path sanitization to send-message.ts recipient parameter
items.approach: In send-message.ts, add allowlist-based input validation for the recipient parameter: recipient must match pattern /^[a-zA-Z0-9._@-]+$/. Reject any value containing characters outside this allowlist (includes ../, /, \0 and all other special characters). Return a clear error message on rejection.
items.acceptance: recipient must match pattern /^[a-zA-Z0-9._@-]+$/. Test validates rejection of ../, /, \0 and all non-allowlist characters. Error message is clear on rejection. typecheck and tests pass.

repair_phases.name: Evidence Artifacts + Approval Placeholders
repair_phases.description: Create 7 missing evidence artifacts for implementation phases that were completed without evidence, and populate the 2 placeholder approval artifacts with real content after this repair cycle completes.

items.title: Create 7 missing evidence artifacts
items.approach: Create evidence artifacts for phases 1.2 (credential_migration), 1.3 (adapter_consumer_complete), 1.6a (data_migration_fk_audit), 1.6b (data_migration_complete), 1.7a (sql_runtime_cleanup), 1.7b (sql_schema_dedup), 1.7c (sql_import_cleanup). Each artifact follows the rig.relay.phase_completion.v1 schema pattern. Verify each artifact against its schema.
items.acceptance: All 7 evidence artifacts exist, validate against their schema, and accurately reflect the completed implementation for each phase.

items.title: Populate approval artifact placeholders with real content
items.approach: After R1-R3 repairs are complete and this repair plan artifact is validated, update red_team_approval.v1.json and repair_plan_approval.v1.json with real content: replace PLACEHOLDER_DIGEST_REPLACE_ME, PLACEHOLDER_BOUNDARY_REPLACE_ME with actual values. Populate reviewer_set, decision_rationale, attack_surface, findings_fixed, residual_defects. Set real issued_at timestamps.
items.acceptance: Both approval artifacts have real values. red_team_approval reflects actual stress findings (coordination tables, DuckDB, FK audit, send_message). repair_plan_approval reflects actual repairs applied. Both validate against their JSON Schema definitions.

blocking_defects: R1 must complete before R4.2 (approval placeholders require repair results)

acceptance_criteria.AC1: BOTH the coordination tables are created on startup (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS) AND write.ts/edit.ts have graceful fallback when checkPathReserved fails. Writes succeed in SQLite default configuration without coordination_reservation table errors.
acceptance_criteria.AC2: DuckDB subprocess starts with -readonly flag; write queries rejected at process level
acceptance_criteria.AC3: DuckDB uses connection pooling with maxConnections=4, idleTimeout=30000ms. 8 concurrent queries produce ≤4 duckdb processes. No subprocess-per-query beyond pool limit. Pool recycles failed connections.
acceptance_criteria.AC4: FK audit detects violations beyond 1000 rows per table; all violations reported
acceptance_criteria.AC5: send_message recipient validates against allowlist pattern /^[a-zA-Z0-9._@-]+$/; ../, /, \0 and all non-allowlist characters rejected with clear error message
acceptance_criteria.AC6: All 7 missing evidence artifacts exist and validate
acceptance_criteria.AC7: Both approval artifacts populated with real content and validate against schemas
acceptance_criteria.AC8: Boundary declaration: this repair targets only files changed in R1-R4 and their direct callers/consumers. All repairs survive a phase-scoped hostile pass with no new blocking findings within this boundary.

order_dependencies: R1 must complete before R4.2 (approval placeholders require known repair state)
R2 and R3 are independent and may run in parallel
R4.1 (evidence artifacts) may run in parallel with R1-R3
R4.2 must be the last phase executed
repair_limit_rationale: 8 total repairs across 4 phases, 3 per-phase ceiling. Each phase has 1-2 focused items. Evidence artifacts (R4.1) bundle 7 file creates into one item.

rollback_strategy.method: Per-phase checkpoint commits. Each phase produces an independent set of file changes that can be reverted individually.
rollback_strategy.r1_rollback: git revert the R1 checkpoint commit. write.ts and edit.ts fallback changes are purely additive and safe to revert. coordination DDL fix is additive (CREATE IF NOT EXISTS).
rollback_strategy.r2_rollback: git revert the R2 checkpoint commit. DuckDB hardening is purely additive (new args, new pooling logic). Existing DuckDB init code remains intact.
rollback_strategy.r3_rollback: git revert the R3 checkpoint commit. Both FK audit and send_message changes are scoped replacements within their respective files.
rollback_strategy.r4_rollback: git revert the R4 checkpoint commit. All created files are new evidence artifacts — removal is safe. Approval placeholders are new values in existing files, revertable.
rollback_strategy.full_rollback: git revert all 4 checkpoint commits in reverse order (R4 -> R3 -> R2 -> R1).

out_of_scope: Coordination table full schema audit — only the missing coordination_reservation table fix is in scope
DuckDB WASM or browser-based DuckDB — desktop-native subprocess hardening only
New coordination features beyond write tool fallback and DDL fix
Architectural changes to Ralph scanner or agent-facing tools
Full DuckDB performance optimization — only concurrency safety (pooling) and security (-readonly) are in scope
